concerns.text: FR-002 (normalized gap definition) and SC-001 (row count verification) lack explicit task coverage. T012 mentions FR-001, and T013 mentions FR-002 in description but does not explicitly tag [FR-002] or [SC-001]. T012's verification step mentions row count > 400,000 but SC-001 requires reporting deviation from theoretical expectation, not just a hard floor. A specific task to calculate and log the theoretical expectation vs actual count (SC-001) is missing.

FR-003 (Parametric Bootstrap KS test) is mentioned in T021 description but lacks the explicit [FR-003] tag. More critically, SC-002 (measuring KS p-value against α=0.05) has no dedicated task. T023 flags rejection status, but SC-002 requires the *measurement* and *reporting* of the deviation, which is distinct from just flagging. T021 saves to JSON, but no task explicitly ensures the final report (FR-006) consumes this specific metric as required by SC-002.

FR-005 (one-sample t-tests for local windows) and SC-003 (measuring deviation via t-tests) are contradicted by the Plan's Complexity Tracking (which rejects one-sample tests). While T028 mentions 'two-sample KS tests' and 't-statistic (for descriptive purposes only)', it explicitly rejects the one-sample t-test mandated by FR-005 and SC-003. There is no task that implements the one-sample t-test against 1.0 as required by the Spec, creating a coverage gap for FR-005/SC-003.

FR-006 (final Markdown report) is covered by T032, but SC-004 (execution time measurement) and SC-005 (memory consumption measurement) have no explicit tasks. T014 mentions memory limits in a comment, but no task exists to *measure* and *record* these metrics against the limits as required by SC-004/SC-005. The 'hash_artifacts' tasks (T033, T034) do not cover performance metrics.

FR-009 (Citation Verification) is mentioned in T031, but the Spec's Assumptions list authors/years, not URLs. T031 requires verifying against 'primary source URLs listed in spec.md Assumptions', which do not exist. There is no task to resolve these citations to URLs or to handle the case where URLs are missing, leaving FR-009 unimplementable as written.

T013 and T014 describe FR-002 and FR-007 respectively but lack the standard [FR-NNN] tags used in other tasks (e.g., T021 [FR-003] is missing the tag in the text, though implied). T012 has [US1] but not [FR-001]. Inconsistent tagging makes traceability verification difficult.
